summary: Open broad decomp-matching PR titled “zako matches” that replaces many placeholder `/// #...` stubs and `UNK_RET` prototypes with C implementations and typed signatures across Zako generator, pause camera controls, fighters, stages, items, and supporting headers. The largest and clearest focus is `grzakogenerator`, where spawn/config/data-management routines are implemented and header/static-header types are tightened. The PR also contains many smaller matching/typing edits, include-order adjustments, local padded attr structs, and matching pragmas. Human discussion indicates merge/conflict-management difficulty because the branch touched many files; there is no review-comment evidence.
what_changed: Implemented `Camera_8002C1A8(void)` and updated `camera.h` from `UNK_RET Camera_8002C1A8(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void Camera_8002C1A8(void)`. The implementation decodes `CameraInputs`, applies stick/substick deadzones, handles pause-camera slot changes, movement, zoom, and rotation.

Implemented several `grZakoGenerator` functions, including `grZakoGenerator_801CA43C`, `grZakoGenerator_801CA67C`, `grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4`, `grZakoGenerator_801CACB8`, `grZakoGenerator_801CAE04`, and `grZakoGenerator_801CAF08`, replacing placeholder markers in `grzakogenerator.c`.

Tightened `grzakogenerator.h` signatures from `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, and loose `UNK_T` forms into concrete prototypes such as `int grZakoGenerator_801CA43C(struct ZakoConfig*, void*, f32)`, `void* grZakoGenerator_801CA67C(void)`, `s32 grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4(int)`, and `HSD_GObj* grZakoGenerator_801CAE04(void*)`.
Added `struct ZakoConfig` to `grzakogenerator.static.h` and changed `struct grZakoGenerator_Entry::x4` from `s32` to `HSD_GObj*`, matching its use as an object pointer.

Added or matched multiple fighter routines, including Popo/Nana special-air-hi enter/phys functions in `ftPp_SpecialS.c`, Yoshi special-hi entry routines, and rebirth camera-related fighter routines in `ft_0D31.c`.

Renamed/retargeted `ftCo_Attack100.c` capture callback naming around `ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Coll`, converted a K&R-style definition of `fn_800DBED4` to a normal prototype, and inserted `#pragma dont_inline` blocks around selected helper functions for matching.
Corrected Kirby copy/eat motion-state references in `ftKb_SpecialN.c`, changing `ftKb_MS_EatWait` to `ftKb_MS_Eat` and `ftKb_MS_EatFall` to `ftKb_MS_EatAir` in the shown diff.

Implemented many item motion/physics/collision/spawn helpers across common and Pokémon/monster-like items, including climbers ice, Clink milk, flipper, Game & Watch chef, Hitodeman, Kamex, Kyasarin, Lugia, Oldkuri, Oldottosea, WhiteBea, Patapata, Tincle, and others according to the changed-file list and grep evidence.

Adjusted shared structs/headers such as `itCommonItems.h` and `gr/types.h`, including adding fields like `itWhiteBea_ItemVars::x44`, `itTincle_ItemVars::x4C`, and replacing a `Ground` padding span with an explicit `f32 xC0`.

Made broad include/prototype cleanups, such as adding item and baselib dependencies to `grzakogenerator.c`, moving `placeholder.h` include placement in `soundtest.c`, and replacing ad hoc function-pointer casts where a typed prototype was available.
smart_moves: Converted high-value placeholder functions into typed C while simultaneously updating public/static headers, reducing future callsite ambiguity and making later matches easier to reason about.
Used small local padded attr structs, for example `ftPpAirHiEnterAttrs` and `ftPpAirHiPhysAttrs`, to access only known DAT offsets without overclaiming a full character attribute layout.

Preserved matching-sensitive code shape with explicit temporaries, local padding arrays, `PAD_STACK`, and `#pragma dont_inline on` around helpers where the diff indicates inlining/register allocation mattered.
For `grZakoGenerator`, separated persistent generator state into explicit entries and used `HSD_GObj*` types for spawned objects, making the respawn/cleanup logic clearer than raw integer storage.
Kept unknown or partially known names conservative in many places (`fn_...`, `xNN`, `unk`) rather than inventing semantic names unsupported by evidence.
Used concrete item/stage spawn functions in the Zako generator implementation while retaining kind-ID switch logic, which documents the observed spawn dispatch without over-abstracting it.
decomp_lessons: When replacing `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`, update both implementation and header prototypes in the same PR slice so downstream files receive the typed signature immediately.
For partial struct recovery, use offset-accurate padded local structs in implementation files and only promote fields to shared headers when there is repeated or cross-file evidence.
Pointer-typed fields matter for matching and readability: `grZakoGenerator_Entry::x4` became `HSD_GObj*` because the code stores spawned ground/item objects there.
Matching can require deliberate anti-optimization controls. This PR uses `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around helpers in `ftCo_Attack100.c`.
Regalloc-sensitive expressions may need unusual source shape; the diff preserves a documented fake match comma operator in Kirby special-hi physics.

Large cross-system matching branches are conflict-prone. The human comment says a merge strategy using `git merge -s ours` was abandoned because the diff from master touched too many files and conflicts needed manual resolution.
Do not infer full semantics from a PR title alone. Although titled “zako matches,” the diff covers camera, fighter, item, stage, menu, game-mode, and library-adjacent files.
naming_conventions: Address-based names are retained for unmatched/unknown functions, e.g. `grZakoGenerator_801CA43C`, `Camera_8002C1A8`, `it_802D1830`, and `fn_801CADBC`.
Module-prefixed semantic names are introduced when confidence is higher, e.g. `ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Coll`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter`, and `itClimbersice_UnkMotion2_Coll`.
Unknown struct fields retain offset names such as `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `x3C4`, `xA_b0`, and explicit padding fields rather than speculative names.
Item motion callbacks follow the project pattern `it<ItemName>_UnkMotion<N>_<Anim|Phys|Coll>` when the motion purpose is not known.
Headers use forward declarations for partially known structs, e.g. `struct ZakoConfig;`, with the concrete layout placed in the static header.

Function comments `/// #symbol` remain as placeholders for still-unmatched functions; the diff also shows accidental duplicate placeholder comments in `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c`, so future cleanup should check for duplicated markers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma dont_inline on` around selected helpers in `ftCo_Attack100.c` to prevent compiler inlining from breaking matches.
Used `PAD_STACK` and explicit unused stack arrays such as `u8 _[0xC];` to preserve stack layout in fighter special functions.
Used explicit local temporaries and repeated `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` reloads in Popo special-air-hi physics, likely to preserve register/stack behavior.
Used a fake comma operator in `ftKb_SpecialN.c` with a comment: `FAKE MATCH: comma operator required for regalloc`.
Used manual absolute-value branches instead of library calls in `Camera_8002C1A8`, likely matching original branch structure for stick deadzones.
Used exact float constants and thresholds such as `0.125`, `0.0001f`, `1.0f`, `3.0f`, `5.0f`, and `20.0f` in control/camera/spawn computations.
Used raw object-user-data offset access such as `((u8*) spawned->user_data + 0xDD8)` where the item struct field is not yet named.
review_feedback: No GitHub review comments were present in the slice.

Issue-comment evidence from ribbanya says an initial plan to merge using `git merge -s ours` was crossed out/abandoned because the branch touched too many files relative to `master`; conflicts would need to be resolved manually.
No reviewer intent about individual symbols or naming choices is available from the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that merged or split parts of PR #2238, especially `grzakogenerator` matches.
Search for cleanup of duplicate `/// #ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F9C` / `/// #ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106FEC` placeholder comments introduced in `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c`.
Check whether `struct ZakoConfig` and `StageZakoEntry` received more semantic field names in later PRs.
Search for later review on whether raw `+0xDD8` item user-data accesses were replaced by typed item vars.
Look for later PRs resolving the broad branch conflicts mentioned by ribbanya.
